title: quercus × comptoniae
intro: Quercus × comptoniae, also known as Compton oak, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae), resulting from the cross between the English oak (Quercus robur) and the sessile oak (Quercus petraea). The medicinal properties of Quercus × comptoniae include its high content of tannins, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Quercus × comptoniae is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
